text.title: Frontend Design Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying frontend-design. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Five 'variants' are colour permutations of one layout; stakeholder picks based on logo placement.
antipattern.root-cause: Author shortcut variant generation; structural distinctness skipped.
antipattern.fix: Reject variant sets where >=2 differ only in colour or font. Enforce: nav pattern + density must differ across the set.

antipattern.symptom: Selection made from Figma frames; production rendering shows different content density and broken responsive at picked viewport.
antipattern.fix: Block selection until screenshots in target viewports exist. Make screenshot step the gate.

antipattern.symptom: Engineer started component coding before tokens were defined; rebranding requires touching every component.
antipattern.root-cause: Tokens felt like 'design system' work and got deferred.
antipattern.fix: Enforce tokens-before-components order. Refactor offending components to reference tokens.

antipattern.symptom: Storybook upgrade breaks all stories; team blocked for a day.
antipattern.root-cause: Un-pinned versions; addon upgrade pulled incompatible Storybook major.
antipattern.fix: Pin Storybook + addons exactly. Schedule explicit upgrade work, not opportunistic.
